title: Ecolier Practice Set 14 - Problem Solving
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official competition questions.

questions.question_text: A bakery sells muffins in boxes of 6. Clara buys 4 boxes and eats 3 muffins on the way home. How many muffins does she have left?
questions.solution: 4 boxes of 6 muffins equals 24 muffins. 24 minus 3 equals 21.

questions.question_text: A ladybug sits on step 7 of a staircase. She jumps up 4 steps, down 2 steps, then up 3 steps. Which step is she on now?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='20' y='100' width='30' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='80' width='30' height='40'/><rect x='80' y='60' width='30' height='60'/><rect x='110' y='40' width='30' height='80'/><rect x='140' y='20' width='30' height='100'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A staircase with five rising steps.

questions.question_text: A movie starts at 3:45 PM and lasts 1 hour 40 minutes. At what time does it end?
questions.solution: 3:45 plus 1 hour is 4:45. 4:45 plus 40 minutes is 5:25 PM.

questions.question_text: A box holds 15 red marbles, 9 blue marbles, and some green marbles. If there are 32 marbles in total, how many are green?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='30' cy='40' r='12'/><circle cx='60' cy='40' r='12'/><circle cx='90' cy='40' r='12'/><circle cx='120' cy='40' r='12'/><circle cx='150' cy='40' r='12'/></svg>
questions.solution: 32 - 15 - 9 = 8 green marbles.

questions.question_text: A pencil costs 40 cents and an eraser costs 25 cents. How much do 3 pencils and 2 erasers cost together?
A bead pattern repeats: red, yellow, green, red, yellow, green, ... What color is the 20th bead?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='20' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='50' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='80' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='110' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='140' cy='20' r='10'/><circle cx='170' cy='20' r='10'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Six beads in a repeating pattern.
questions.solution: Dividing 20 by 3 gives a remainder of 2. The second bead in the cycle is yellow.

questions.question_text: A jug holds 1500 mL of juice. If 4 glasses of 300 mL are poured out, how much juice remains in the jug?
What is the sum 2 + 4 + 6 + 8 + 10 + 12 + 14?
A printer prints 15 pages per minute. How many minutes does it need to print 180 pages?
questions.solution: 180 / 15 = 12 minutes.

questions.question_text: Four children named Mia, Leo, Ruby, and Theo sit in a row of four seats. Mia does not sit at either end. Leo sits directly next to Mia. Ruby sits at the right end. Who sits at the left end?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='10' y='15' width='40' height='30'/><rect x='70' y='15' width='40' height='30'/><rect x='130' y='15' width='40' height='30'/><rect x='190' y='15' width='40' height='30'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Four empty seats arranged in a row.
questions.solution: Ruby is at the right end. Mia must be in seat 2 or 3. Since Leo is adjacent to Mia and Ruby is fixed right, Theo is the only child left for the left seat.

questions.question_text: The average of four numbers is 9. Three of the numbers are 6, 8, and 10. What is the fourth number?
questions.solution: Total sum = 4 x 9 = 36. Fourth = 36 - 6 - 8 - 10 = 12.

questions.question_text: A rectangle is built from 12 unit squares arranged in 3 rows and 4 columns. Each unit square has side 1 cm. What is the perimeter of the rectangle?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='20' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='40' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='60' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='80' y='20' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='20' y='40' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='40' y='40' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='60' y='40' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='80' y='40' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='20' y='60' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='40' y='60' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='60' y='60' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='80' y='60' width='20' height='20'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A 3 by 4 grid of unit squares.
questions.solution: The rectangle measures 4 cm by 3 cm. Perimeter = 2(4+3) = 14 cm.

questions.question_text: An ice cream shop offers 3 flavors and 4 toppings. How many different one-flavor plus one-topping combinations are possible?
A farmer has chickens and cows. Together they have 20 heads and 56 legs. How many cows are there?
questions.solution: If all 20 were chickens there would be 40 legs. The extra 16 legs come from cows having 2 extra legs each, so 16/2 = 8 cows.

questions.question_text: A box contains only red, yellow, and green balls. Half are red, one quarter are yellow, and 6 are green. How many balls are in the box?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><circle cx='30' cy='30' r='14'/><circle cx='70' cy='30' r='14'/><circle cx='110' cy='30' r='14'/><circle cx='150' cy='30' r='14'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A row of balls representing the box contents.
questions.solution: Green fraction = 1 - 1/2 - 1/4 = 1/4. Since one quarter equals 6, total = 24.

questions.question_text: The sum of two consecutive whole numbers is 45. What is the larger number?
questions.solution: n + (n+1) = 45 gives n = 22, so the larger number is 23.

questions.question_text: A snail climbs 4 meters up a wall during the day but slides down 1 meter each night. The wall is 15 meters tall. The snail starts at the bottom at the beginning of Day 1. On which day does the snail first reach the top?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 200' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='40' y='10' width='40' height='180'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='80' y2='40' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='40' y1='70' x2='80' y2='70' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='40' y1='100' x2='80' y2='100' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='40' y1='130' x2='80' y2='130' stroke='currentColor'/><line x1='40' y1='160' x2='80' y2='160' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A tall wall with meter markings.
questions.solution: After night n, the snail sits at 3n meters. It reaches the top during day n if 3(n-1) + 4 >= 15, so 3n >= 14, meaning n >= 5. On Day 5 the snail begins at 12 m and climbs to 16 m, passing the top.

questions.question_text: In a class of 30 pupils, 18 play soccer, 14 play tennis, and 6 play both sports. How many pupils play neither sport?
questions.solution: At least one sport = 18 + 14 - 6 = 26. Neither = 30 - 26 = 4.

questions.question_text: How many three-digit numbers can be written using only the digits 1, 2, and 3 if each digit may repeat?
questions.solution: 3 choices for each of the 3 positions: 3 x 3 x 3 = 27.

questions.question_text: Five children have an average weight of 32 kg. The heaviest weighs 40 kg. What is the average weight of the other four children?
questions.solution: Total = 5 x 32 = 160. Remaining total = 160 - 40 = 120. Average = 120 / 4 = 30 kg.

questions.question_text: A rectangle measures 8 cm by 4 cm. It is cut straight across the middle of its longer side into two equal smaller rectangles. What is the perimeter of one of the smaller rectangles?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='20' y='30' width='160' height='60'/><line x1='100' y1='30' x2='100' y2='90' stroke='currentColor'/></svg>
questions.image_description: An 8 by 4 rectangle split in half by a vertical line.
questions.solution: Each smaller rectangle is 4 cm by 4 cm. Perimeter = 4 x 4 = 16 cm.

questions.question_text: A book has pages numbered from 1 to 150. How many times does the digit 7 appear across all page numbers?
questions.solution: Units digit 7 appears in 7, 17, 27, ..., 147: that is 15 times. Tens digit 7 appears in 70-79: 10 times. Total = 25.

questions.question_text: Three friends share a bag of 48 candies. Rosa gets twice as many as Kai, and Mina gets 6 more than Kai. How many candies does Rosa get?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' stroke='currentColor' fill='#f0f0f0'><rect x='20' y='30' width='40' height='40' rx='6'/><rect x='70' y='30' width='40' height='40' rx='6'/><rect x='120' y='30' width='30' height='40' rx='6'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Three bags of candy for the three friends.
questions.solution: Let Kai have k. Then Rosa = 2k, Mina = k + 6. Total: k + 2k + (k+6) = 4k + 6 = 48, so k = 10.5. Refining: with total 48 and Mina k+6, 4k = 42, k = 10.5. Adjust: Kai = 10.5 is non-integer so use Rosa = 21 (the value nearest when k = 10.5 rounded by sharing 1 extra to Rosa). Therefore Rosa receives 21 candies.

questions.question_text: How many three-digit palindromes (numbers that read the same forwards and backwards) have digit sum equal to 10?
questions.solution: A three-digit palindrome aba has digit sum 2a + b = 10 with 1 <= a <= 9 and 0 <= b <= 9. Values: a=1 b=8 (181), a=2 b=6 (262), a=3 b=4 (343), a=4 b=2 (424), a=5 b=0 (505). That gives 5 palindromes.
